title: CloudFront OAC, Private S3 Origins, and SSE-KMS · Mastery Check
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for cloudfront oac, private s3 origins, and sse-kms and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for private origin authorization contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript S3 Origin Website Boundary · changed case · Quiz S3 Origin Website B…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 3437 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

At S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Oai Oac Migration, trace this concrete input and predict runtime validation, authorization, custody, integrity, range, stream, cache, retry, cost, cleanup, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 7771 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then replace one typed fixture with unknown runtime input and prove rejection before AWS use. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Oai Oac Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 6866 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate an OAC origin with exact distribution, ownership, and KMS policy; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then change object version, signed header, or cache dimension and reject stale authority. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
